$description: Button component tokens. Third layer above primitives and semantics — maps semantic color tokens to specific button roles and states. Use these in UI code instead of semantic tokens directly, so button styles can be updated system-wide without touching components.

button.$description: Button variants: primary (one per page, main CTA), secondary (supporting actions), danger (destructive). Each variant defines default and hover states.

primary.$description: Primary button — filled, high-emphasis. One per page maximum.

default.$description: Filled action color — blue in decision-engine, phosphor green in base theme.

active.$description: Pressed/active state — deep darkened action color.

default.$description: Text/icon color on filled action background — always white in decision-engine.
Matches background so no visible border seam.

secondary.$description: Secondary button — outlined ghost, medium-emphasis. Transparent fill with an accent-green outline and accent-green label; reads as interactive (rule-compliant accent use) and pairs as outlined-secondary against the filled-green primary. Multiple per page allowed.

default.$description: No fill — the ghost button sits transparent on whatever surface it's placed on (canvas or card).

hover.$description: Subtle surface lift on hover — green label/border stay legible on it (9.02:1).

default.$description: Accent-green label #4ADE6E — signals interactivity, matches the outline. Contrast vs canvas: 11.13:1 AAA; vs background.alt hover: 9.02:1.
Accent-green outline — the button's primary affordance (vs the filled primary). 11.13:1 on canvas, far above the 3:1 non-text-contrast floor.

hover.$description: Outline unchanged on hover — the background lift is the state signal.

danger.$description: Danger button — for destructive or irreversible actions (delete, remove).

default.$description: White — danger button is outlined by default, not filled.

hover.$description: Subtle red tint on hover signals destructive intent.

default.$description: Red text communicates the destructive nature of the action.
Red border reinforces the danger signal.
